summary: review_only triage: is_secure=False effective_total_issues=2 (raw=2, false_pos=0, duplicates=1, review_duplicates=1)

sast_review.reason: pickle.loads is called on untrusted data (decoded from user-supplied Base64). The subsequent dict/keys check does not prevent code execution during deserialization.

raw.summary: The code uses pickle.loads on untrusted user input (Base64-decoded payload). Although the code checks that the deserialized object is a dict with allowed keys, this check occurs AFTER pickle.loads has already executed arbitrary code during deserialization. This is a critical insecure deserialization vulnerability (CWE-502). The SAST findings are true positives.

sast_review.reason: pickle.loads is called on untrusted data (decoded from user-supplied Base64). The subsequent dict/keys check does not prevent code execution during deserialization.
Importing pickle is flagged as a risk because it is used unsafely in the code (line 13). This is the same root cause as B301.
